channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Google TV Devices to Feature 'Free TV' Button, Taking Users Directly to 150 Free Channels - Tech News
item.description: All new Google TV devices will feature a 'Free TV' button on included remotes, taking users directly to a hub with over 150 free channels.
item.dc:creator: james.davidson@futurenet.com (James Davidson), TechRadar

item.title: Prague Zoo joins the effort to ensure the survival of a rare insect once considered extinct
item.description: The Prague Zoo has joined an international effort to ensure the survival of a rare insect that had been considered extinct for more than 80 years.
item.dc:creator: Karel Janicek, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China’s rare earth export controls threaten enterprise IT hardware supply chains
item.description: China has announced immediate export controls on seven more rare earth elements critical to enterprise IT hardware manufacturing, firing a fresh salvo in the ongoing tech trade war.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta launches AI family Llama 4
item.description: Over the weekend, Meta took the opportunity to launch Llama 4, a new series of AI models trained on a large amount of text, images and videos.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 7 risk management rules every CIO should follow
item.description: Risk is inescapable. Look around and you’ll see technological, economic, and competitive obstacles that CIOs must not only handle, but defeat.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: An Intel-TSMC deal could reshape x86 future and enterprise chip supply chains
item.description: Intel is reportedly in advanced discussions with TSMC to form a joint venture that could potentially reshape the future of the x86 platform and the global semiconductor landscape.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IT job postings lowest in over a decade
item.description: Postings for IT jobs are on the wane, having dropped considerably from pandemic peaks. Perhaps more concerning for IT pros is the sense that demand for their services may be at a 10-year low.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: A US-Russian crew of 3 arrives at the International Space Station
item.description: NASA astronaut Jonny Kim and two Russian crewmates have arrived at the International Space Station on board a Russian spacecraft.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Visual Studio Code stabilizes agent mode
item.description: Visual Studio Code 1.99, the latest release of Microsoft’s popular code editor, is now available. Highlights of the update center on GitHub Copilot agent mode, Next Edit Suggestions, and Copilot chat. Developers can access Visual Studio Code 1.99, also known as the March 2025 release, through code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: This silent, fanless mini PC looks like a very refined and expensive Hi-Fi unit, and I'm surprised by how affordable it is
item.description: The Quiet PC UltraNUC Pro 14 Fanless is designed for business users seeking a high-performance, noise-free workstation with a premium look.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Redbox goes up for auction following a wild bankruptcy saga
item.description: Redbox is getting ready for one final sale. The defunct DVD rental chain’s assets, and those of its corporate siblings Crackle and Chicken Soup for the Soul Entertainment, are being auctioned off in New York later this month, according to a court filing published Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Shopify CEO Tobi Lütke: AI is now a ‘fundamental expectation’ for employees
item.description: Shopify CEO Tobi Lütke shared an internal memo on X Monday that stressed the importance of using AI effectively in daily tasks. In fact, he wrote, using AI is now a “fundamental expectation” of Shopify employees.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Europe considers new tariffs that could punish tech companies like Google, Meta, and Apple
item.description: As the European Union looks at how best to respond to Donald Trump’s trade war, officials are considering further escalating things, taking them to a point where it could really hurt America’s Big Tech companies.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: 'Battlestar Galactica' is 'even more relevant now,' star says
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: At 50, Microsoft highlights AI and Copilot as the company’s future
item.description: At a special 50th anniversary event on Friday, Microsoft executives reflected on the company’s storied past and on how it’s now reinventing itself for an AI-focused future.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took only seconds for the judges on a New York appeals court to realize that the man addressing them from a video screen — a person about to present an argument in a lawsuit — not only had no law degree, but didn't exist at all.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A cyber call to arms
item.description: Without a call to arms, the United States is dangerously vulnerable today, and still more tomorrow, to increasingly sophisticated and brazen cyberattacks. The post A cyber call to arms first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: How internet search algorithms could help critical infrastructure
item.description: Researchers found the algorithms that rank pages in internet searches also can help planners understand how to prevent failures in electrical systems. The post How internet search algorithms could help critical infrastructure first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Why Trump's tariffs are so bad for Big Tech
item.description: President Trump’s sweeping new slate of tariffs is poised to strike a blow at the tech industry, as massive import taxes on China and Taiwan disrupt trade flows that are central to tech firms’ business models.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: DHS plans to slash HQ workforce, including AI experts
item.description: DHS is planning sweeping workforce cuts across its Management Directorate, with targets including the AI Corps and the CX Directorate. The post DHS plans to slash HQ workforce, including AI experts first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Musk will remain 'a friend and an adviser' to Trump after leaving DOGE: Vance
item.description: Vice President Vance said Thursday that billionaire Elon Musk will remain a “friend and an adviser” to the White House after Musk departs from his role as a special government employee next month.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nintendo's Switch 2 is bigger and better than its predecessor of eight years past as the video game developer pushes to bring its brand into “a new era" where social experiences are at the forefront.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Future of Robotics: Meet Unitree's Agile G1 Humanoid Robot
item.description: Unitree's G1 humanoid robot is making headlines with its impressive agility, acrobatic skills, and affordability.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meta is fined by Turkey after refusing to restrict content on Facebook and Instagram
item.description: Meta said it has been hit with a hefty fine for resisting Turkish government demands to limit content on Facebook and Instagram.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: FBI warns about new extortion scam targeting sensitive data
item.description: According to the FBI, cybercriminals are sending extortion letters, threatening to release victims' sensitive information unless a ransom is paid.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why no one is challenging Trump's executive order that keeps TikTok running
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After TikTok was banned in the United States earlier this year, President Donald Trump gave the platform a reprieve, barreling past a law that was passed in Congress and upheld unanimously by the Supreme Court that said the ban was necessary for national security.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Detroit production of Mozart opera turns its female characters into automatons
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Audiences at the Detroit Opera House expecting their performance of “Cosi fan tutte" to begin with the overture may be surprised to hear instead a product launch from a tech company CEO.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SILVERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4
